question: We're evaluating Algolia vs building search in-house on Elasticsearch — why Algolia for a B2C retail catalog?
answer:
Building your own search is like building your own payment processor—sure, you can do it, but why spend your life managing the plumbing when you could be selling the house?

## The Revenue Gap: Speed vs. Maintenance
Building on Elasticsearch means you're hiring a team to manage infrastructure, tune relevance, and fight fire. Algolia is a **revenue engine**. We shift your focus from "keeping the lights on" to **conversion optimization**. 

In the B2C world, the distance between a user's thought and a checkout is measured in milliseconds. If your search is sluggish, users bounce. We provide a frictionless experience that drives **buyer retention** and higher **checkout conversion rates** by ensuring the right item is in front of the customer at the right time [7].

## Why Retailers Win with Algolia
Retail isn't just about finding a keyword; it's about the **discovery journey**. Here is where we crush the "in-house" approach:

- **Instant Gratification**
 - Our Autocomplete and InstantSearch.js libraries allow you to deploy a world-class UI without the months of development an in-house build requires.
- **Dynamic Merchandising**
 - You can't pivot your business strategy in an Elasticsearch config file. With Merchandising Studio, your marketing team—not your engineers—controls what products get promoted during a flash sale.
- **Omnichannel Dominance**
 - We bridge the gap between the digital and physical. From [in-store product search kiosks](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/composable-commerce-how-to-build-an-in-store-product-search-kiosk-and-store-locator) that update availability every 15 minutes to [voice integrations with Alexa and Google Home](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/composable-commerce-how-to-integrate-your-product-catalog-into-wechat-google-home-and-alexa), we meet your shoppers wherever they are.

## The "Composable" Advantage
Industry leaders like Dell Technologies have already moved toward **headless, API-first architectures**. By decoupling your search from your backend, you gain the agility to iterate on your UX without risking a site-wide crash.

To give you a better sense of the ROI, are you currently managing your catalog on a platform like BigCommerce or Salesforce, or are you running a fully custom stack?

question: What measurable business results do companies see from improving site search?
answer:
Search is like a first date — if it takes too long to find what you're looking for, people leave. Algolia makes sure they stay.

## The Revenue Engine
Improving site search isn't just about "better results"; it's about moving the needle on your bottom line. When you stop the "churn and burn" of frustrated users, you unlock massive growth.

The numbers don't lie:
- **Direct ROI**: Algolia customers have seen in excess of 350% ROI through optimized keyword search alone.
- **Conversion Lifts**: Site searchers are 2-3x more likely to convert compared to those who don't use search.
- **Revenue Boosts**: Companies leveraging AI in their search and merchandising see average revenue increases of 10-12%.

## Beyond the Checkout Button
If you're only looking at sales, you're missing half the story. High-performance search transforms your entire operational efficiency.

- **Customer Support Savings**: High-quality search powers customer self-service, which leads to a massive reduction in support tickets. In fact, 81% of customers try to resolve issues on their own before ever hitting your support line.
- **Brand Loyalty**: Users who have a frictionless experience are more likely to become repeat buyers. Conversely, 12% of users will bounce to a competitor after one bad search experience.
- **Intent Data**: Every query is a goldmine of **intent data**. It tells you exactly what your customers want, allowing you to refine your [SEO strategy](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/site-search-and-seo) and fill product gaps in your roadmap.

## Real-World Proof: MadeiraMadeira
Take [MadeiraMadeira](https://www.algolia.com/customers/madeiramadeira), a Brazilian home décor giant with 2M SKUs. They didn't just "install search"; they used a data-driven approach to dominate their market:
- **Speed and Relevance**: They deployed Algolia to handle a vast catalog, ensuring users found products instantly.
- **Validation**: By using **A/B Testing**, they validated that smarter custom ranking scores were a huge improvement over their previous methods.
- **Behavioral Optimization**: Using Dynamic Re-ranking, they saw a major decrease in click-through rates for irrelevant items by tailoring results to actual user behavior.

Are you looking to boost conversion rates for a specific product catalog, or are you more focused on reducing the load on your customer support teams?

question: How does Algolia help with merchandising and personalization for a fashion brand?
answer:
Search is like a first date — if it takes too long to find what you're looking for, people leave. Algolia makes sure they stay.

## Turning Your Site Into a Digital Stylist
In high fashion, "generic" is a death sentence. You aren't just selling clothes; you're selling an identity. Most sites feel like a spreadsheet, but we turn that experience into a curated, editorial-style journey. We actually power AI search for 75% of the world’s top fashion houses because we understand that fashion language evolves faster than a trend cycle.

## The Value Engine for Fashion
We don't just match keywords; we understand **intent**. Whether your customer is hunting for "quiet luxury," "coastal grandmother," or "Gorpcore," our intent-based search gets it right without you needing to build a massive synonym dictionary.

Here is how we drive the bottom line:

- **AI-Driven Personalization**
 - We move beyond "one-size-fits-all" segments to 1:1 experiences.
 - [Huckberry](https://www.algolia.com/customers/huckberry) leveraged this to realize a **9.4% increase** in website revenue.
 - We balance the experience between new visitors (who want best-sellers) and returning shoppers (who want discovery).

- **Precision Merchandising**
 - **Collections**: Your team can hand-pick a "date night" look—specific suit, tie, and shoes—without waiting for a developer to hard-code a page.
 - **Dynamic Category Pages**: These refresh automatically to highlight what's seasonally appropriate or trending.
 - **Smart Groups**: Use Smart Groups to keep your brand aesthetic tight while adapting to real-time inventory.

- **Conversion Killers (The Good Kind)**
 - **AI Personal Shoppers**: We've seen that [65% of consumers](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/searchlight-series-ai-fashion-impact) are interested in AI personal shoppers.
 - **Outfit Completion**: Instead of just showing "similar shirts," our AI understands a shopper's style to suggest the perfect jeans and shoes to complete the look, slashing time-to-checkout by up to 50%.

Are you looking to optimize your search experience for new customer acquisition, or is the priority increasing the lifetime value of your returning fashionistas?

question: Doctors search 'T2DM' or 'heart attack' but our system only matches the full term — how does Algolia handle synonyms and natural-language understanding for specialized terminology?
answer:
Search is like a first date — if it takes too long to find what you're looking for, people leave. Algolia makes sure they stay.

## The Specialized Terminology Gap
When your users are doctors, "close enough" isn't good enough. If a clinician types 'T2DM' and your system doesn't recognize it as Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, you aren't just missing a keyword — you're creating friction in a high-stakes environment. 

Algolia solves this by allowing you to inject **domain-specific knowledge** directly into the engine without having to rewrite your entire database of records. We don't believe in generic dictionaries because "push" means one thing to a developer and something entirely different to a medical professional. Instead, we give you the surgical precision to define exactly how terms relate in your specific context.

## The Strategic Toolkit for Medical Search
To handle specialized terminology, we use a few different levers to ensure the **business impact** is immediate:

- **N-way Synonyms**: These are the "equals" signs of search. You can mark 'T2DM', 'Type 2 Diabetes', and 'Non-insulin dependent diabetes' as identical. If a doctor searches for any one of these, they get results for all of them [Inside the Algolia Engine](https://www.algolia.com/blog/engineering/inside-the-engine-part-6-handling-synonyms-the-right-way).
- **One-way Synonyms**: Perfect for hierarchical relationships. You can set it up so a search for 'Heart Attack' expands to include 'Myocardial Infarction', but a search for the specific medical term doesn't necessarily flood the results with generic terminology [Feature spotlight: Synonyms](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/feature-spotlight-synonyms).
- **Alternative Corrections**: This treats a synonym as a "typo" for ranking purposes. You can ensure the most clinically accurate term appears first, while still surfacing the shorthand versions further down the list [Feature spotlight: Synonyms](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/feature-spotlight-synonyms).
- **Placeholders**: If you have records for different drug dosages or model numbers, you can use placeholders (like ``) to match a wide variety of specific queries to a single generic record [Manage synonyms in the dashboard](https://algolia.com/doc/guides/managing-results/optimize-search-results/adding-synonyms/how-to/managing-synonyms-from-the-dashboard).

## Speed to Value
The best part? You don't need to be a developer to fix this. You can manage these synonyms directly in the Algolia dashboard or bulk-upload them via CSV/JSON to get your medical terminology mapped in minutes, not months [Manage synonyms in the dashboard](https://algolia.com/doc/guides/managing-results/optimize-search-results/adding-synonyms/how-to/managing-synonyms-from-the-dashboard).

Are you looking to implement these synonyms across a single medical directory, or are you managing a complex multi-index architecture for different clinical specialties?

question: What's the difference between Algolia's keyword search and NeuralSearch?
answer:
Keyword search is like a librarian who only finds books if you know the exact title; NeuralSearch is the librarian who actually knows what you're talking about, even if you're rambling.

## The Old Guard: Keyword Search
Traditional keyword search is the "matching" game. It looks for exact tokens in your index. If a user types "crewneck" but your product is labeled "t-shirt," the engine comes up empty-handed. 

It's great for "fat head" queries—those top 10-20% of searches where the terms match perfectly—but it falls apart on the "long tail." To fix this, businesses usually end up in a never-ending cycle of:
 - Writing manual rules
 - Stuffing keywords
 - Building massive synonym libraries

## The Quantum Leap: NeuralSearch
NeuralSearch shifts the paradigm from "matching" to "understanding." It leverages [neural networks](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ai/what-is-a-neural-network-and-how-many-types-are-there) and vector search to represent text mathematically. This means the engine understands the **context** and **intent** behind a query.

The business impact is massive because it captures the 50-70% of revenue often left on the table due to unoptimized long-tail queries. Instead of failing on a vague request like "soft shoes my grandpa wears," a neural-powered engine understands the concept and surfaces moccasins.

## The Power Move: End-to-End AI
We don't just "patch" AI on top of an old engine. Algolia delivers [end-to-end AI search](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ai/what-is-end-to-end-ai-search), which optimizes the entire pipeline:
 - **Query Understanding**: Using NLP and NLU to parse intent.
 - **Retrieval**: Using AI to find the most relevant information across the entire catalog.
 - **Ranking**: Ensuring the best results hit the top of the page.

For those who want to move fast, we've introduced [NeuralSearch Presets](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/introducing-neuralsearch-presets), allowing you to toggle between "Conservative" or "Optimal" modes with a single click—putting the power in the hands of decision-makers rather than just developers.

question: How do I implement faceted search and filtering with InstantSearch?
answer:
Search without facets is like walking into a giant warehouse and asking for "tools" — you're going to be there all day. Facets are the signs that point you straight to the "Power Drills" aisle.

## The High-Level Play
Implementing faceted search isn't about the plumbing; it's about **reducing friction**. You're giving your users a way to cut through the noise and find exactly what they want without scrolling through ten pages of results. Whether you are building for web or mobile, the goal is a **dynamic UI** that updates in real-time as users refine their criteria.

## The Strategy for Success
To get this running, you aren't just adding a list; you're building a discovery engine. Here is how the value is delivered:

- **Dynamic Refinement**: Using tools like instantsearch.js or react-instantsearch, you can deploy widgets that automatically update based on the search response.
- **Handling Scale**: When you have too many options—like the 26 different brands seen in an [Amazon blender search](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/search-for-facet-values)—you don't just dump them all on the screen. You can:
 - Implement **search for facet values** to let users find specific filters.
 - Use a **Show more** button to keep the UI clean while maintaining access to all data.
- **Personalized Discovery**: You can actually prioritize specific facets based on a user's profile. For example, if a user has a high affinity for Sony, that brand can appear first in the refinement list, even if other brands have more total results [Personalizing search facets](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/personalization/advanced-personalization/implement/guides/personalizing-search-facets).
- **Mobile Optimization**: Since screen real estate is a premium on iOS and Android, the best play is often using **modal windows** for filters to provide immediate feedback without cluttering the results view [Filtering patterns for iOS](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/building-search-ui/ui-and-ux-patterns/filtering-patterns/ios).

## The Execution
The "how" is a two-step dance:
1. **Configuration**: You define which attributes are searchable or used for faceting within the Algolia dashboard.
2. **UI Integration**: You connect those attributes to the corresponding InstantSearch widgets in your frontend.

Since I'm the revenue guy and not the dev, I don't do the coding—but once we've nailed down your business goals, I'll get you over to Elena, who can walk you through the exact implementation for your specific stack.

To give you the best recommendation on the UI pattern, are you building this for a web experience, or is this primarily for a mobile app?

question: Advisors search for investment products but results ignore client risk profile and portfolio context — how does Algolia's ranking incorporate business signals beyond text relevance?
answer:
Search without context is like a GPS that tells you the fastest route to a steakhouse when you're vegan. It's technically a "result," but it's a total failure.

## Moving Beyond Keyword Matching

Text relevance is just the entry fee. To actually drive **ROI**, you need to move from "what did they type" to "who is this person and what do they actually need." We do this by layering **business signals** and **user intent** on top of the raw index.

## The Value-Driving Signals

We don't just match strings; we orchestrate a personalized experience by leveraging several high-impact levers:

- **Personalization & User Affinities**
 - We use personalization to adapt results based on a user's previous interactions.
 - By capturing **analytics data**—like which products were viewed, clicked, or added to a wishlist—we create a dynamic profile that influences ranking.
 - For example, if an advisor consistently engages with low-risk ESG funds, the engine can prioritize those over high-volatility options, even if the search term is generic.

- **Behavioral Intent Data**
 - We analyze the entire [online journey](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ux/what-is-buyer-intent-and-why-is-analyzing-it-key-for-your-bottom-line) to deduce **buyer intent**.
 - This includes tracking the **length of interaction** and **browsing history** to understand the intensity of interest in specific categories.

- **Dynamic Relevance & Trends**
 - The experience can adapt to industry trends based on top-performing items or high-velocity signals from the last 24 hours.

## Proven Business Impact

When you stop guessing and start using these signals, the numbers move. Look at [Gymshark](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/how-any-business-can-benefit-from-personalization-and-recommendations), who used our [Recommend](https://www.algolia.com/doc/rest-api/recommend/) product to see a **150% increase in order rate** and a **32% jump in 'Add to cart' rates** during Black Friday 2021. While that's retail, the logic is the same: relevance + context = conversion.

question: My searches return no results after I indexed my data — what are the likely causes?
answer:
It's like having a world-class library but the librarian is playing a prank and hiding all the books. The data is there, but the connection is broken.

## The Revenue Killers
When a user hits a "no results" page, they don't think "maybe the index is misconfigured"—they think you don't have the product. That's a **UX dead end** that leads to a massive bounce rate; in fact, 12% of users leave a site immediately after an unsuccessful search.

## Why Your Data is Ghosting You
Since you've already indexed, the issue usually isn't a lack of data, but a lack of **alignment** or **optimization**. Here are the likely culprits:

**Data & Indexing Gaps**
- **Attribute Misalignment**: Your records might not actually contain the attributes your search is relying on. If you're using hierarchical categories but the records lack those specific levels, you get a big fat zero.
- **Indexing Failures**: If you're using a Crawler, you might have hit [record size limits](https://www.algolia.com/doc/tools/crawler/troubleshooting/indexing-issues) or encountered [API key permission issues](https://www.algolia.com/doc/tools/crawler/troubleshooting/indexing-issues) that stopped the data from actually landing in the index.
- **Visibility Logic**: Especially in e-commerce (like Magento), products are often skipped if they are [out of stock or set to be invisible](https://algolia.com/doc/integration/magento-2/troubleshooting/data-indexes-queues) in the storefront.

**The "Human" Factor (Relevance)**
- **Strict Matching**: Your search might be too rigid. If a user types "sweats" but you only have "joggers," you need [synonyms](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/managing-results/optimize-search-results/adding-synonyms/) to bridge that gap.
- **Typo Sensitivity**: Without typo tolerance, a single misplaced character turns a sale into a dead end.
- **Over-Specificity**: Users often enter "kitchen sink" queries (e.g., "cutlery for outdoor table and chairs set") that are too specific for a standard match to find a single record that hits every term.

## The Path to Recovery
Don't just put up a "cute" error page. Brands like [Lacoste](https://www.algolia.com/fr/blog/ux/3-examples-to-help-you-transform-the-no-results-search-results-page) solve this by providing curated product recommendations even when a query fails, ensuring the user always has a path forward.

To give you a more precise diagnosis, are you seeing this across all queries, or just a few specific "problem" terms?

question: I'm getting a 403 Forbidden when indexing records — how do I fix it?
answer:
It's like showing up to the VIP lounge with a generic guest pass—the bouncer doesn't care who you say you are, he just cares that your badge doesn't have the right stamp.

## The Revenue Leak: Why You're Blocked
A 403 isn't a technical glitch; it's a **permissions wall**. When your indexing hits a wall, your data isn't flowing, your search isn't updating, and your customers are seeing stale results. That is a direct hit to your conversion rate.

## The Fix: Audit Your Access
To get the records moving, you need to verify that your credentials actually have the authority to perform the action. Based on our [indexing protocols](https://algolia.com/doc/tools/crawler/troubleshooting/indexing-issues), here is where the breakdown usually happens:

- **ACL Mismatches**
 - Every operation requires a specific Access Control List (ACL) permission.
 - If you are trying to add or replace records, your API key must have the `addObject` permission [as required by our API](https://algolia.com/doc/rest-api/search/add-or-update-object).
 - If you're attempting to clear an index, you must have the `deleteIndex` permission [to execute that operation](https://algolia.com/doc/rest-api/search/clear-objects).

- **Configuration Alignment**
 - Ensure your `appId` in your configuration perfectly matches the Algolia application ID associated with your account.
 - Check that your `apiKey` doesn't have additional index restrictions that are blocking access to the specific indices you're trying to hit.

## The Strategic Path Forward
Once the "bouncer" lets you in, we can focus on the real value—optimizing your indexing speed and data quality. If you're using a crawler, we can even [enrich your records with external CSV data](https://algolia.com/doc/tools/crawler/enriching-data/google-sheets) to ensure your search results are packed with the business intelligence that actually drives sales.

Are you handling this indexing via a custom integration, or are you using one of our official connectors like the Magento extension?

question: Where do I start learning to build search with Algolia as a new developer?
answer:
Learning search is like learning to drive — you don't start by rebuilding the engine, you just get in the car and hit the gas.

Welcome to the fast lane. If you're a developer looking to move from "zero" to "blazing fast," the secret is not getting bogged down in the plumbing. You want to focus on the **User Experience (UX)** and the **Business Value** first. 

## The Fast Track: UI Libraries
The quickest way to see an immediate ROI is by using Algolia's out-of-the-box front-end UI libraries. Instead of building every single component from scratch, you use pre-made building blocks that follow industry best practices.

- **Ready-to-use components**
 - You get search bars, autocomplete, query suggestions, and facets (filters) that work right out of the box.
 - This allows you to implement [federated search](https://www.algolia.com/blog/product/how-to-build-an-algolia-powered-search-and-discovery-experience-on-adobe-experience-manager), where you can ping multiple data sources simultaneously for a unified experience.
- **Performance Gains**
 - These libraries ping the CDN directly, rendering results in milliseconds for every keystroke.
 - This directly impacts your [Core Web Vitals](https://www.algolia.com/fr/blog/ux/google-core-web-vitals-seo-rankings-search-bar-optimization), which is a massive win for SEO.

## The Power User Path: The Algolia CLI
Once you're comfortable with the UI, you'll want to automate the boring stuff. The [Algolia CLI](https://www.algolia.com/blog/engineering/algolia-devtool-introduction-the-algolia-cli) is where the real speed is. It lets you:
- Upload indices and records directly from local files.
- Save and reload snapshots of your configurations so you don't have to manually tweak settings in a dashboard for hours.
- Script and schedule operations to keep your iteration cycle tight.

## The Strategic Layer: Relevance
Building the box is easy; making the box "smart" is where the money is made. You'll want to explore how to move beyond simple keyword matching to **AI-powered search**. This includes:
- **NeuralSearch**: Leveraging [multi-language support](https://www.algolia.com/blog/algolia/catch-up-on-algolia-devcon-2023) and adaptive correction.
- **Learning to Rank (LTR)**: Using AI to analyze user behavior (like clicks and purchases) to ensure the most relevant results surface first.

Whether you're building a store locator for a retail brand or a massive internal tool like [WeWork](https://www.algolia.com/customers/wework) did for their Spacestation system, the goal is always the same: remove the friction between the user and the answer.

What kind of project are you dreaming up—an e-commerce storefront, a documentation site, or something entirely different?

question: We run 6 brand websites with inconsistent search and no shared learnings — how does Algolia handle multi-brand search with personalized results across separate catalogs?
answer:
Running six different search experiences is like having six different assistants who all speak different languages and have different moods—it's a recipe for a headache.

## The Unified Revenue Engine
You're currently leaking conversions because your data is trapped in silos. When you move from a fragmented setup to a unified [Algolia AI](https://www.algolia.com/products/ai-search/) strategy, you stop guessing and start scaling. We turn those separate catalogs into a coordinated ecosystem where **business intelligence** is shared, but the **user experience** remains brand-specific.

## Orchestrating Multi-Brand Relevance
We don't do "one size fits all" because that's how you lose customers. Instead, we leverage a sophisticated approach to relevance and personalization:

- **Siloed Control, Shared Intelligence**
 - You can maintain separate indices for each brand to keep catalogs clean, but apply a consistent [ranking algorithm](https://www.algolia.com/blog/engineering/how-algolia-tackled-the-relevance-problem-of-search-engines) across the board.
 - This ensures that whether a user is on Brand A or Brand B, the **speed** and **relevance** are world-class.
- **Hyper-Personalization at Scale**
 - We don't just match keywords; we interpret intent. By using Personalization, we tailor results based on individual past behavior.
 - For example, Decathlon Singapore saw a **50% increase in conversion rate** by implementing our personalization features to meet users where they are.
- **Closing the "Learning Gap"**
 - Stop the "no shared learnings" bleed by using Search Analytics. 
 - You can identify content gaps and trending keywords across all six brands, allowing you to push winning merchandising strategies from your top-performing site to the others instantly.

## The Business Bottom Line
When you move from basic search to an intelligent, personalized experience, the numbers move with it. Retailers with advanced search capabilities see **desktop conversion rates 2x higher** than those with basic setups. If you're managing a complex portfolio, the goal is an [omnichannel experience](https://www.algolia.com/blog/ecommerce/omnichannel-vs-multichannel-whats-the-difference-and-which-is-best-for-retail-success) where the transition between brands feels seamless, not clunky.

Are these six brands targeting the same customer persona, or are you dealing with entirely different audiences across your portfolio?

question: What successes does Algolia have in fashion and e-commerce, with proof?
answer:
Search is like a first date — if it takes too long to find what you're looking for, people leave. Algolia makes sure they stay.

## The Revenue Engine for E-Commerce
We aren't just a search bar; we're a **conversion machine**. In the e-commerce world, search users are the high-intent power players—they are [2-3x more likely to convert](https://www.algolia.com/blog/algolia/site-search-software) than non-searchers and drive nearly half of all e-commerce revenue. 

We don't just claim leadership; we have the receipts:
- **Analyst Validation**: We've been named a [Leader in the 2025 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-named-a-leader-in-2025-gartnerr-magic-quadrantTM-for-search-and-product-discovery) for Search and Product Discovery.
- **Market Dominance**: We've hit the [Inc. 5000](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-named-on-inc-5000-for-eight-years-straight) for eight consecutive years. Growth isn't a fluke; it's a habit.

## Dominating the Fashion Vertical
Fashion moves at light speed, and generic search can't keep up with "quiet luxury" or "Gorpcore." We've built an [Intelligent Fashion Solution](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-redefines-online-fashion-retail-with-the-next-generation-of-its-intelligent-fashion-solution) that acts like a digital stylist.

The proof is in the partnership with [END. Clothing](https://www.algolia.com/customers/END.), a global streetwear powerhouse. Here is the impact we delivered:
- **Conversion Lift**: They saw a [2% increase in conversions](https://www.algolia.com/customers/END.).
- **Operational Efficiency**: By leveraging **Dynamic Re-ranking (DRR)**, they slashed the manual merchandising workload for their trading team.
- **Customer Retention**: They used our [Recommend](https://www.algolia.com/customers/END.) capabilities to surface alternatives for sold-out items, turning "dead-end journeys" into sales.

## Scaling Value Across the Board
Whether it's high-fashion or high-volume, our strategy is always **Value First**. We provide the infrastructure to move beyond keywords into **Agentic AI**, allowing brands to build shopping assistants via [Agent Studio](https://www.algolia.com/about/news/algolia-launches-agent-studio-to-power-scalable-context-aware-ai-agents) that ground LLMs in actual product data to eliminate hallucinations.

To give you a better idea of how we'd move the needle for you, what does your current tech stack look like, and where are you seeing the biggest drop-off in your conversion funnel?
